behavior_in_task_description.reason: Instruction names both file paths, class names (SwiftFlowIterator, KotlinFlowSequence, Iterator, KotlinFlowIterator), required snippets (invokeOnCancellation { cancel() }, coroutineScope.launch, _kotlin_swift_SwiftFlowIterator_cancel), and visibility requirements.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction provides literal code to insert, e.g. 'private val coroutineScope: CoroutineScope = CoroutineScope(EmptyCoroutineContext)', 'continuation.invokeOnCancellation { cancel() }', 'coroutineScope.launch {', and '_kotlin_swift_SwiftFlowIterator_cancel(iterator.__externalRCRef())' — essentially stating the fix.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests use regex like '(?:val|var)\s+\w+\s*:\s*CoroutineScope' and check for .cancel() call on any scope, allowing alternative field names.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests only read file contents and apply regex (read_kotlin_file(), read_swift_file(), re.search) — no code execution or behavioral verification.

test_not_tautological.reason: Tests verify specific patterns that correspond to the actual fix; a stub would not pass.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Tests cover coroutineScope field, cancel() using scope, invokeOnCancellation in both states, launch using scope, Swift Iterator class with deinit.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are named with test_ prefix and grouped under '==== FAIL-TO-PASS TESTS ====' comment block.

structured_data_schema.reason: Code fix task, no structured data output.

no_typos.reason: Paths and symbols match between instruction, tests, and solution.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction specifies exact fields to add ('field: private val coroutineScope: CoroutineScope = CoroutineScope(EmptyCoroutineContext)') and exact method calls to include, going beyond identifying the broken component.
